page: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 4345P processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 123.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding approximately 7.71 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 3890 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10%. Passmark memory scores also rank in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance but a weak write speed for large blocks, placing in the bottom 25%. For lightweight LLM inference using a 135M model, text generation is top-tier, while prompt processing is strong.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal configuration offers high resource density, combining fast DDR5 memory and a large local NVMe SSD on dedicated hardware. The primary tradeoff lies in the write memory bandwidth, which is weak compared to its top-tier single-core processing capabilities.

This server is well-suited for workloads requiring high single-thread performance, substantial memory capacity, and fast local storage. Appropriate use cases include database hosting, memory-cached applications, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by an 8-core, 16-thread AMD EPYC 4345P processor running at 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 123.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory and a bundled 3890 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and memory performance, though multi-core execution and large-block memory write bandwidth are average to weak. This hardware profile makes the server highly effective for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and workloads requiring fast single-threaded processing.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC 4345P CPU, 123.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3890 GB NVMe SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-speed DDR5 memory, fast local NVMe storage, and top-tier single-core AMD EPYC processing power.

123.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 3890 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
